name: Data Command
summary: Dense operational dashboard language with metrics, tables, status systems, mono numerals, and controlled high-signal accents.

style_dna.philosophy: Operating-room clarity: show state, trend, risk, and next action quickly.
style_dna.visual_signature: Metric cards, dense tables, status chips, thin dividers, and mono numbers.
style_dna.surface_logic: Light dashboard canvas with white modules and status-coded accents.

palette.accent_policy: Accent colors belong to metrics, status, and comparison only.
palette.semantic_policy: Every semantic color must map to a label or legend.

layout.grid: Dashboard modules, KPI strips, table-first slide bodies, right-side insight rail.
layout.whitespace: Tight but scannable; group by decision layer.
layout.image_treatment: Charts, tables, UI captures, and system diagrams over decorative imagery.

components.buttons: Small operational controls, status chips, tabs.
components.cards: Metric cards with label, value, delta, and tiny context line.
components.tables: Header bands, row dividers, mono numeric columns, status badges.

slides.accent_rule: Use accent colors only for metrics, status, and comparison.

document.heading_rule: Dense headings, strong tables, mono numerals, status labels.

source_inheritance.method: Synthesized from local getdesign.md working corpus.

do: Map every color to a metric or status.
Expose trend, risk, and action together.
dont: Do not use status colors without labels.
Do not make dashboards sparse like brand slides.
Do not hide tables in screenshots.

pptx: Use deterministic HTML/CSS or PPTX text boxes for final text.
Keep one core idea per slide and validate rendered PNG dimensions.
Use tables, numbered evidence, and source notes instead of vague decorative cards.
docx: Translate visual style into Word typography, margins, heading hierarchy, tables, and callouts.
Keep documents printable unless the user explicitly requests PDF-only visual art direction.
carousel: Use high-resolution fixed canvas, mobile-readable text, and safe margins.
Make each frame complete enough to stand alone.
